title: elytrigia repens subsp. arenosa
intro: Elytrigia repens subsp. arenosa, also known as a coastal form of the creeping meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the Elytrigia repens plant, which is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the body. The plant's medicinal properties have been linked to various health benefits, including reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes, as well as improving cognitive function and immune system function. Elytrigia repens subsp. arenosa can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
